steps.title: Introduce the examples
steps.content: The words before the punctuation form a complete independent clause: exemplars of Stigler's law abound. The following material gives examples from mathematics, astronomy, and physics, so a colon should introduce the list after the complete clause.

steps.title: Place the colon correctly
steps.content: A uses commas where a list-introducing mark is needed. B puts the colon after in mathematics, making the first field seem separate from the parallel list. D uses a semicolon after in mathematics, disrupting the series. The colon belongs after abound. The answer is C.
